seoTitle: How to Connect Vantage Broker to TradingView Charts
metaDescription: Link your Vantage trading account to TradingView to trade directly from the chart. Learn to authorize access, connect demo or live accounts, and fix errors.
intro: Connecting your Vantage account to TradingView lets you analyze and place trades from the same chart without switching between platforms. This guide covers logging into Vantage, finding the broker connection panel on TradingView, and authorizing access to link either a demo or live account. It also covers fixing common connection errors like a broker not being available in your region.

steps.heading: Log into your Vantage account
steps.body: Sign into Vantage and make sure your account is verified before attempting to connect it to TradingView.

steps.heading: Open TradingView's broker connection panel
steps.body: In TradingView, go to the section for connecting a broker and search for Vantage among the available options.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Enter your Vantage credentials to authorize TradingView to access your account securely.

steps.heading: Connect a demo or live account
steps.body: Choose whether to link a demo account for practice or your live account for real trading, depending on what you're testing.

steps.heading: Verify the connection and test trading
steps.body: Confirm the broker status shows connected in TradingView, then place a small test trade to make sure execution and balance updates are working.

steps.heading: Fix common connection errors
steps.body: If you see errors like 'broker not available in region' or 'failed to connect account,' check your account type and region settings before retrying.

faq.question: Why does TradingView say Vantage is not available in my region?
faq.answer: This error usually relates to regional restrictions on certain broker integrations, so it's worth confirming Vantage supports your account's region before troubleshooting further.

faq.question: Can I connect both a demo and live Vantage account to TradingView?
faq.answer: Yes, TradingView supports connecting either account type, so you can test with a demo account before switching to live trading.

faq.question: Will my account balance update in real time once connected?
faq.answer: Once the connection is verified, TradingView should reflect real-time balance and leverage updates from your linked Vantage account.

faq.question: What should I do if TradingView fails to connect my Vantage account?
faq.answer: Double-check your login credentials and account verification status on Vantage, then retry the authorization step in TradingView's broker panel.
